headline: Polymarket’s World Cup Bets Hit $3.9 Billion. Now Trump and Zuckerberg Want A Piece.
summary: Prediction markets just had their coming-out party, and the numbers are staggering. $3,996,191,303 has traded on the “World Cup Winner” market as of July 6, 2026, per Polymarket, a single contract that has quietly surpassed the platform’s 2024 US presidential contract (~$4 billion). With the July 19 final still ahead, Bernstein projects total World Cup ... Polymarket’s World Cup Bets Hit $3.9 Billion. Now Trump and Zuckerberg Want A Piece.

headline: The Safest-Looking Yield Trade on Robinhood Might Be This $50 ETF
summary: Scroll through Robinhood’s ETF page and you keep bumping into a boring blue ticker parked near $50 a share that barely moves. That is the WisdomTree Floating Rate Treasury Fund (NYSEARCA:USFR), and it has quietly become one of the most popular parking spots for retail cash in 2026. USFR functions more like a cash-management ETF ... The Safest-Looking Yield Trade on Robinhood Might Be This $50 ETF

headline: 3 Internet Stocks on Our Watchlist
summary: Whether it be online shopping or social media, secular forces are propelling consumer internet businesses forward. But it’s not all sunshine and rainbows as consumer purchasing power can make or break demand. Unfortunately, the market seems to believe stormy skies are ahead as the industry has shed 9.9% over the past six months. This performance is a stark contrast from the S&P 500’s 8% gain.

headline: There's a lot of 'noise' making it harder for investors to read the true state of the stock market
summary: Rarely has so much attention been placed on the mechanical impact of index inclusion, policy-driven investment flows and options-hedging impact.

headline: Chips Get Their Confidence Back, the Dow Cracks 53,000, and Trump Moonlights as a Dell Salesman
summary: Chip and memory stocks broadly rebounded Monday after last week's selloff, helping the Dow close above 53,000 for the first time as Microsoft's latest round of job cuts failed to reassure investors. Analyst upgrades lifted AMD and ASML, President Trump publicly endorsed Dell, a bitcoin miner signed a multibillion-dollar AI leasing deal, and auto-parts retailers sold off hard while EV makers rallied.

headline: ValuEngine Weekly Market Summary And Commentary

headline: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev reflects on his journey from immigrant to CEO
summary: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev reveals what he believes is the secret to America’s success and reflects on his journey as an immigrant to CEO. Explaining why he believes America’s culture of entrepreneurship will continue to drive innovation and the global economy.

headline: Why Robinhood (HOOD) Stock Is Up Today
summary: Shares of financial services company Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) jumped 4.6% in the afternoon session after reports showed that consumer confidence edged up to 91.2. in June.
